key_risks: A hot June 5 employment report or stronger ISM data could push yields higher and trigger a growth-stock selloff before the exit close.
Recent high-beta technology and Korea gains could reverse sharply due to profit-taking after strong trailing returns.
Any negative AI, semiconductor, or mega-cap technology news could disproportionately hurt the concentrated thematic exposure.
A risk-off move from weak macro data could cause the portfolio to underperform the broader S&P 500 due to higher beta.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest short-term relative momentum among liquid technology themes, with recent 7-day and 30-day gains suggesting continued risk-on rotation into software.
Broad AI technology exposure captures the dominant growth and momentum theme while diversifying beyond a single subsector.
Semiconductor sales data were strong and the group retains high AI-linked earnings sensitivity, supported by robust trailing momentum.
Large-cap technology provides liquid beta to the same leadership trend with somewhat broader diversification than thematic funds.
South Korea showed exceptional recent momentum and offers leveraged exposure to global semiconductor and export sentiment.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio is concentrated in the strongest one-week and one-month momentum areas, especially AI, software, semiconductors, and Korea-linked technology. With volatility still moderate and financial conditions easy, trend persistence over a one-week window appears more attractive than defensive positioning despite macro event risk.
rationale_summary: I favor high-beta technology and AI momentum over the S&P 500 for the close-to-close week. The allocation seeks to exploit recent leadership while spreading exposure across software, chips, broad tech, and Korea semiconductor beta.
